children.heading: Committee
children.label: a Committee
children.text: There is hereby established an interagency committee composed of the heads of the Departments of Energy, Commerce, Interior, Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Health and Human Services, the Environmental Protection Agency, the Appalachian Regional Commission, the Farmers’ Home Administration, the Office of Management and Budget, and such other Federal agencies as the Secretary shall designate. In carrying out its functions the committee shall consult with the National Governors’ Conference and interested persons, organizations, and entities. The chairman of the committee shall be designated by the President. The committee shall terminate 90 days after the submission of its report under subsection (c).
the level of assistance provided under this chapter and any other programs related to impact assistance,
the timeliness of assistance in meeting impacts caused by Federal decisions on energy policy as well as private sector decisions, and
the obstacles to effective assistance contained in regulations of existing programs related to impact assistance.

children.heading: Report
children.label: c Report
children.text: Within 1 year after the effective date of this chapter, the committee shall submit a detailed report on the results of such study to the Congress, together with any recommendations for additional legislation it may consider appropriate.
